'''Martin Pearson''' (born 24 October 1971) is a former professional [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played representative level rugby league (RL) for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level for [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (two spells), the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], as a [[Goal (sports)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|fb|ce|so}}, or {{rlp|sh}},<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>Graham Williams, Peter Lush, David Farrar (November 2009). "The British Rugby League Records Book [Page-108…114]". London League Publications Ltd. {{ISBN|978-1-903659-49-6}}</ref> and club level rugby union in France for [[Section Paloise|Pau]] and [[RC Toulonnais|Toulon]].

==Playing career==
===International honours===
Pearson won four [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] between 1998 and 2001, scoring two tries and two goals.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

===Division Two Premiership Final appearances===
Martin Pearson played {{rlp|fb}} and scored four [[Try (rugby)|conversion]]s in [[Featherstone Rovers]]' 20-16 victory over [[Workington Town]] in the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93]] [[Rugby Football League Championship Second Division|Division Two Premiership]] Final at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Wednesday 19 May 1993.<ref name="Rovers Dig Deep To Lift The 1993 RL Premiership">{{cite web|url=http://www.btinternet.com/~Rovers/gpgg11.htm|title=Rovers Dig Deep To Lift The 1993 RL Premiership|publisher=Great Players - Great Games|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121011225606/http://www.btinternet.com/~Rovers/gpgg11.htm|archivedate=11 October 2012}}</ref>

===Career Records===
Martin Pearson holds [[Featherstone Rovers]]' 'The Most Points In A Season' record, with 391-points scored in the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]].
